name: LunchBox
capability: Paneling systems, parametric structures, math surfaces, data/ML utilities and workflow tools for computational design.
installHint: Download the .gha from Food4Rhino and unblock + drop into Grasshopper's Libraries folder (File > Special Folders > Components Folder); on Mac/Rhino 8 install via the Rhino Package Manager. Latest builds target .NET 7.

name: Pufferfish
capability: 330 components for tweens, blends, morphs, averages, transforms and interpolations — parameter/factor-driven shape changing.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unblock the .gha, place in Grasshopper Libraries folder (File > Special Folders > Components Folder). Works on Rhino 5/6/7/8, Windows and Mac.

name: Weaverbird
capability: Topological mesh modeler implementing known subdivision and smoothing operators for designers.
installHint: Download from giuliopiacentino.com/weaverbird (or Food4Rhino), unblock and drop the .gha into the Grasshopper Components/Libraries folder, then restart Rhino.

name: Kangaroo2
capability: Live physics / constraint solver for form-finding, relaxation, collision and interactive simulation via goal objects.
installHint: Bundled with Rhino 6/7/8 by default (appears under the Kangaroo2 tab). For Rhino 5, download from Food4Rhino, unblock and place the .gha in the Grasshopper Libraries folder.

name: Ladybug
capability: Imports EPW weather data to run and visualize sun-path, radiation, comfort and environmental analyses in early design.
installHint: Install the full Ladybug Tools (LBT) bundle, not a bare .gha. Preferred path on Rhino 7/8 is the Pollination one-click installer; otherwise use the Food4Rhino installer, then keep updated with the 'LB Versioner' component.

name: Honeybee
capability: Connects Grasshopper to validated engines (EnergyPlus/OpenStudio, Radiance, THERM) for energy, daylight and thermal simulation.
installHint: Ships inside the Ladybug Tools (LBT) bundle alongside Ladybug; install via the Pollination one-click installer (Rhino 7/8) or the Food4Rhino installer. Requires the backing simulation engines (EnergyPlus, Radiance) which the installer can place.

name: Heteroptera
capability: 112-component toolbox focused on uncertainty/randomness, vector fields, networks/topology, streaming and math utilities.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unblock the .gha (right-click > Properties > Unblock), and place it in the Grasshopper Libraries folder (File > Special Folders > Components Folder), then restart.

name: Dendro
capability: OpenVDB-based volumetric (voxel) modeling — wrap points/curves/meshes into volumes for robust boolean, offset, smooth and blend ops.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unblock and drop the .gha into the Grasshopper Libraries folder. Built on OpenVDB; use the build matching your Rhino version (6/7/8) and architecture.

name: Anemone
capability: Adds looping / recursion to Grasshopper (classic, fast and internalized loop modes) for iterative and feedback definitions.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unblock the .gha and place it in the Grasshopper Libraries folder (File > Special Folders > Components Folder), then restart Rhino. 10 components by Mateusz Zwierzycki.

name: Fox
capability: Series of algorithms for graphs, aggregations and iso-surfaces (a faster single-element alternative to Wasp for aggregation).
installHint: Download the .gha and .dll from Food4Rhino, unblock both, and place them in the Grasshopper Components folder (File > Special Folders > Components Folder), then restart. Author: Petras Vestartas. NOTE: no authoritative source enumerates Fox's exact component names, so hero components are intentionally omitted rather than guessed.

name: Parakeet
capability: Algorithmic 2D/3D pattern and network generation — tilings, Islamic/star patterns, venation, growth and aggregation.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unblock the .gha and place it in the Grasshopper Libraries folder, then restart Rhino. Components are grouped under categories like Tilings, Pattern Generation and M.C. Escher.

name: Mesh+
capability: Topological mesh editing/creation producing effects from faces and point sets via subdivision and normal-based 3D operations.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino (page /app/mesh; grasshopperdocs slug 'meshplus'), unblock and place the user-object/.gha files in the Grasshopper Libraries folder. Supports Rhino 4/5/6/7/8 on Windows; recent build fixes Rhino 8 / .NET Core compatibility.

name: Elk
capability: Generates map geometry and topographic surfaces from OpenStreetMap (OSM) and USGS/SRTM elevation data for site and context modeling.
installHint: Download the .gha from Food4Rhino, place it in Grasshopper > File > Special Folders > Components Folder, unblock the file (right-click > Properties > Unblock), and restart Rhino. Only 3 components total.

name: Human
capability: Extends Grasshopper's ability to create and reference Rhino geometry (lights, blocks, text, hatches) and to read/modify document attributes like layers, materials, and lineweights.
installHint: Install the .gha into the Grasshopper Components folder and unblock it. Delete any older HDTReference.gha, TreeFrog.gha, and Human.gha first to avoid duplicate-load conflicts. Restart Rhino.

name: MetaHopper
capability: Meta-programming toolkit that queries and manipulates Grasshopper components/parameters on the canvas (enable/disable, rename, move, set properties, get connected objects) for definition automation and cleanup.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, place the .gha in the Components folder, unblock, and restart Rhino. Components appear under a 'MetaHopper' tab.

name: Telepathy
capability: Wireless sender/receiver that pipes data anywhere on the canvas via shared key names (supports many-to-one, one-to-many, and wildcards) to eliminate long wires and modularize definitions.
installHint: In Rhino 8, install directly from the Package Manager (Yak) — search 'Telepathy' (v1.0.1). For Rhino 6/7, download the .gha from Food4Rhino, drop into the Components folder, unblock, and restart.

name: Octopus
capability: Multi-objective evolutionary solver (SPEA-2 / HypE) that searches many goals at once to produce a Pareto front of trade-off solutions; includes evolutionary/supervised-learning network tools.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, unzip, place all files in the Components folder, unblock each .gha/.dll, and restart Rhino. The main 'Octopus' component is a draggable solver window; wire sliders to its genome input and fitness values to its objectives.

name: Opossum
capability: Single- and multi-objective black-box optimization solver using model-based RBFOpt/RBFMOpt and evolutionary CMA-ES/NSGA-II; tuned to find good designs with few expensive simulation evaluations.
installHint: In Rhino 8, install from the Package Manager (Yak) — search 'Opossum'. Otherwise download from Food4Rhino, place in the Components folder, unblock, restart. The solver is a single dialog component; connect sliders to its variables and an objective number to its input.

name: Wallacei
capability: Evolutionary multi-objective optimization and analytics engine (NSGA-II + K-means clustering) with detailed in-canvas charts and phenotype selection/reconstruction tools.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, place the files in the Components folder, unblock, and restart Rhino. 'Wallacei X' is the main solver window (double-click to open its UI); the analytics components read its output.

name: Bison
capability: Landscape-architecture / terrain toolkit for creating, analyzing, editing, and annotating terrain meshes (slope, aspect, watershed, cut/fill, contours, sections).
installHint: Download the .ghpy from Food4Rhino and save it into Grasshopper > File > Special Folders > Components, then restart Rhino — it loads automatically. (No unblock needed for .ghpy.)

name: Nautilus
capability: Large 150+ component utility/geometry library by Laurent Delrieu covering spirals, mazes, reaction-diffusion, tessellations, geodesics, single-line paths, noise fields, and mesh tools.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino and place files in %AppData%\Roaming\Grasshopper\Libraries (the Components folder), unblock, and restart Rhino. Note: some components require a paid license (Level 1 ~€50/2yr); unlicensed ones still run.

name: Tarsier
capability: Treats point clouds as a first-class Grasshopper data type — construct/deconstruct, filter, voxellize, reduce, clip, and KD-tree search clouds; also streams from Kinect/OpenNI2 depth sensors.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino, place the .gha in the Components folder, unblock, and restart Rhino. Kinect/OpenNI2 'Vision' components require the corresponding sensor drivers/SDK installed.

name: FlexHopper
capability: GPU-based, particle physics simulation (NVIDIA Flex) for fluids, rigid/soft bodies, cloth, inflatables, spring systems, and custom constraints inside Grasshopper.
installHint: Download from Food4Rhino (or GitHub HeinzBenjamin/FlexCLI), place all files in the Components folder, unblock, restart. Requires Rhino 6 64-bit and a DirectX 11-capable NVIDIA/AMD GPU — will not run without compatible hardware.

name: Cocoon
capability: Marching-cubes isosurface mesher by David Stasiuk that wraps points, curves, and breps into smooth metaball/blobby meshes with adjustable cell size, radius, and strength.
installHint: Cocoon is NOT on Food4Rhino — download the .gha from the developer/community mirror (see grasshopperdocs page), place it in the Components folder, unblock, and restart Rhino. Only 6 components total.
